text: In the relatively short span of time since Large Language Models (LLMs) such as OpenAI’s ChatGPT came onto the scene, it has presented numerous opportunities and choices for scientists and professionals. This paper attempts to contribute to the AI & Public Policy discussion by considering some of the possible implications of incorporating the advanced natural language processing and data analysis capabilities into the policy-making process. On the positive side, this technology can assist in uncovering intricate language and communication patterns within societies, aid in processing large amounts of unstructured information, facilitate policy-makers in designing predictive models for forecasting policy outcomes and provide them with around-the-clock decision-making support. Moreover, applying this technology to a range of domains may help in increasing efficiency and equality by uncovering biases that human beings are not consciously aware of. However, there are many negative points to consider in the context of applying this technology in governance, which includes the black box effect of generative AI and possible context omission in the output that could be triggered by the underlying biases in the data used for the training of this technology. In total, this paper argues that ChatGPT and its equivalents have a two-fold effect on governance. They present numerous choices and opportunities for governments, policy-makers and citizens in relation to both policy-making and decision-taking. On the one hand, the use of this technology is very likely to revolutionize and develop new ways of decision-taking in favour of policy-makers. On the other hand, it also presents many risks that should be addressed through careful management and appropriate transparency in the context of administrative governance.
explanation: The passage has a polished, generic academic tone with broad AI-related points that are typical of AI-generated text. However, the nuanced governance discussion could also come from a human author, so the determination is uncertain.
